title: Understanding the Problem
text: To find the acceleration, we need to know the initial velocity, final velocity, and the distance over which the velocity changes. What are these values in the problem?

title: Identify Known Values
text: The initial velocity is $$0.600$$ $$\frac{m}{s}$$, the final velocity is $$0$$ $$\frac{m}{s}$$, and the stopping distance is $$2.00$$ mm. Remember to convert the distance to meters.

title: Convert Units
text: Convert the stopping distance from millimeters to meters. $$1$$ mm $$=$$ $$0.001$$ $$m$$.

title: Calculate Acceleration
text: Use the formula $${v_f}^2={v_i}^2+2a d$$ to find the acceleration, where $$v_f$$ is the final velocity, $$v_i$$ is the initial velocity, a is the acceleration, and $$d$$ is the distance.

title: Express Acceleration in Terms of g
text: Once you have the acceleration, express it as a multiple of g, where $$g=9.80$$ $$\frac{m}{s^2}$$.

title: Final Calculation
text: Divide the calculated acceleration by g to express it in terms of g.
